principle: Update governance is this declared config, not manual steps: the ARCHBP-110 guard consumes it, root appliers (ARCHBP-111/112) materialize it, and changing governance means changing this reviewable file.

holds.why: kernel swap removes the running kernel and forces a reboot window
holds.apply_mechanism: apt-mark hold via the ARCHBP-111 root applier; released when no session holds os-update-hold

holds.why: kernel companion packages ride the same swap
holds.apply_mechanism: apt-mark hold via the ARCHBP-111 root applier

holds.why: kernel companion packages ride the same swap
holds.apply_mechanism: apt-mark hold via the ARCHBP-111 root applier

holds.why: restarts snap infrastructure under live sessions (2026-07-21 19:44)
holds.apply_mechanism: apt-mark hold via the ARCHBP-111 root applier

holds.why: restarts the login/user service the desktop session depends on (2026-07-21 19:44)
holds.apply_mechanism: apt-mark hold via the ARCHBP-111 root applier

gate.mechanism: os-update-hold lease in the T8 host control plane (scripts/os-update-observer.mjs hold/release/gate-check)
gate.pre_invoke_hook: generated DPkg::Pre-Invoke snippet (scripts/os-update-observer.mjs snippet) — installed under /etc/apt/apt.conf.d by ARCHBP-111 (root)
gate.decision: while the hold lease is live AND this gate is enabled, every apt/dpkg run fails closed before touching packages; with the gate disabled here, the same hold is advisory only

reboot.requires: explicit operator intent via the ARCHBP-112 root applier (Unattended-Upgrade::Automatic-Reboot=false + notify-and-confirm path)

observability.events: scripts/os-update-observer.mjs events — live apt history, risk-classified against os_update_lifecycle_map.json
observability.timers: scripts/os-update-observer.mjs timers — apt-daily / apt-daily-upgrade last+next fire
